sentences.text: Hans said he valued the Miller's good opinion more than his silver buttons, so he picked all his primroses and filled the Miller's basket.
The Miller said goodbye to Hans as he walked up the hill with the plank on his shoulder and the big basket in his hand.
Little Hans said goodbye and started digging happily because he was excited about the wheelbarrow.
The next day, Hans was fixing some honeysuckle on the porch when he heard the Miller calling him.
